description: Use this API to return an array of complex values for subsystem multi-terminal dc lines. This API routine was first introduced in release 30.3.0. ierr, xarray = amultitrmdccplx(sid, ties, flag, string)
python_syntax: ierr, xarray = amultitrmdccplx(sid, ties, flag, string)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: Defines the bus subsystem to be used. (input; -1 by default).

parameters.name: ties
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem multi-terminal dc lines to include (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem multi-terminal dc lines to include. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: An array of NSTR elements specifying NSTR of the following strings indicating the multi-terminal dc line quantities desired. (input; no default allowed).

return_values.name: xarray
return_values.description: An array of NSTR*DIM elements specifying Is an array of at least NSTR columns, each of which contains DIM elements. (output).

sid.description: to instruct the API to assume a subsystem containing all buses in the working case.
valid bus subsystem identifier. Valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. Subsystem SID must have been previously defined.

ties.description: for interior subsystem multi-terminal dc lines only.
for subsystem tie multi-terminal dc lines only.
for both interior subsystem multi-terminal dc lines and tie multi-terminal dc lines.

flag.description: for only in-service multi-terminal dc lines.

string.description: ac power losses (in MW and Mvar).
ac power losses (units determined by power output options).

error_codes.description: DIM, and hence the size of XARRAY, is not large enough.
